Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 : Search Slack for internal discussions about the stipend fraud audit using conversations_search_messages with queries like "stipend", "TechComfort", "fraud", or "receipt". The agent should discover messages in #finance (Marcus Thorne announcing holds on two pending reimbursements STIP-2026-0094 for Andre Washington $89 and STIP-2026-0097 for Jordan Ekwueme $45) and in #engineering (Dmitri Volkov's detailed audit findings about duplicate receipt hashes and PDF template matches across three client accounts).
OE 2 : Search emails for stipend-related discussions using search_emails with queries like "stipend", "receipt", "audit", or "TechComfort". The agent should find: Dmitri's initial audit report to Marcus identifying three flagged TechComfort Supply receipts, Marcus's reply confirming QuickBooks payment statuses and placing holds, Marcus's email to Mina asking her to verify Kevin Tran's receipt with Rachel Whitfield, and Marcus's email to Emeka about Andre Washington and Jordan Ekwueme.
OE 3 : Search Airtable Stipend Transactions using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the Stipend Transactions table to identify flagged submissions on Mina's accounts. The agent should find: Kevin Tran (Vectral) $89 keyboard, Approval Status "Approved" but flagged with Duplicate Hash audit note (same SHA-256 as Andre Washington's), and Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) $45 monitor stand, Approval Status "Pending Approval" with PDF Template Match flag. The agent should correctly scope to Mina's accounts only (Vectral and BrightLoop), recognizing that Andre Washington (Mosaic) is Emeka's account.
OE 4 : Search CRM deals for Mina's accounts using crm_search_deals with dealname="Vectral" and dealname="BrightLoop" (or first obtain company_id via crm_search_companies with name="Vectral Systems" / name="BrightLoop Analytics", then use crm_search_deals with company_id="company_vectral" / company_id="company_brightloop"). The agent should find: deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (at_risk stage renewal negotiation), deal_brightloop_q2 (qualifiedtobuy — Jordan Ekwueme relocation, expansion signal: "4-5 more relocations this year"), and deal_brightloop_apr2026 (closedwon Simone Richter + Marcus Webb April moves, $11,350).
OE 5 : Search emails for any inbound messages from client HR contacts about delayed payments or stipend issues, using search_emails with queries targeting Rachel Whitfield or Tessa Moreno or "delayed" or "reimbursement". The agent should discover that Rachel Whitfield cited the Kevin Tran receipt flag as one of her grievances in the Vectral Q3 renewal negotiation context not as a standalone stipend delay complaint. No separate inbound complaint from Tessa Moreno about stipend delays exists.
OE 6 : Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne, Tessa Moreno, and David Chen using contacts_search_contacts. The agent should find: marcus.thorne@moveops.com (Head of Finance), tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com (Head of People Ops at BrightLoop), and david.chen@moveops.com (Customer Engagement Lead).
OE 7 : Check BrightLoop's April relocation records in Airtable using airtable_search_records on the Relocations table with field_name "Company" and value "BrightLoop". The agent should find five BrightLoop relocations with April move dates: Jordan Ekwueme (San Diego → Boston, Status: Completed, April 25–26), Simone Richter (Chicago → Boston, Status: In Progress, rush move April 4–6), Marcus Webb (Atlanta → Boston, Status: In Progress, April 14–18, vehicle shipping via Road Runner Auto Transport), Jordan Whitfield (Minneapolis → Denver, Status: In Progress, April 21–23), and Preethi Nair (Atlanta → Raleigh, Status: In Progress, April 21–23). Note: an older completed Marcus Webb record (Atlanta → Denver, Feb 2026) also exists but is not an April move.
OE 8 : Log risk engagement notes on affected CRM deals using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE". The agent should create notes on at least the Vectral and BrightLoop company records (company_ids: company_vectral and company_brightloop) documenting the stipend hold impact — which employees are affected and the risk to the client relationship.
OE 9 : Send Marcus Thorne an email asking for the client-facing timeline on the stipend holds, via send_email from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com. The email should explain which of Mina's clients are affected (Vectral Kevin Tran; BrightLoop Jordan Ekwueme), and request a timeline for when holds will be reviewed/released so Mina can set expectations with client contacts.
OE 10 : Send Tessa Moreno a check-in email via send_email from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com. The email should cover the status of BrightLoop's April relocations (Jordan Ekwueme, Simone Richter, Marcus Webb, Jordan Whitfield, and Preethi Nair) and include language that keeps the expansion conversation going referencing the "4-5 more relocations" signal or upcoming plans.
OE 11 : Create a calendar event for a call with Tessa Moreno using calendar_add_calendar_event with attendees including mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com, a title referencing BrightLoop, and a reasonable date/time.
OE 12 : Brief David Chen on everything via send_email from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com (or alternatively via conversations_add_message in a relevant Slack channel such as #customer-engagement, channel_id C002). The briefing should cover: (a) which of Mina's accounts are affected by the stipend holds (Vectral Kevin Tran; BrightLoop Jordan Ekwueme), (b) the risk to each account relationship, (c) BrightLoop April move status, and (d) the expansion opportunity with BrightLoop.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1 : Search Slack for internal discussions about the stipend fraud audit using conversations_search_messages with queries like "stipend", "TechComfort", "fraud", or "receipt". OE 2 : Search emails for stipend-related discussions using search_emails with queries like "stipend", "receipt", "audit", or "TechComfort". OE 3 : Search Airtable Stipend Transactions using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the Stipend Transactions table to identify flagged submissions on Mina's accounts. The agent should correctly scope to Mina's accounts only (Vectral and BrightLoop), recognizing that Andre Washington (Mosaic) is Emeka's account.
OE 4 : Search CRM deals for Mina's accounts using crm_search_deals with dealname="Vectral" and dealname="BrightLoop" (or first obtain company_id via crm_search_companies with name="Vectral Systems" / name="BrightLoop Analytics", then use crm_search_deals with company_id="company_vectral" / company_id="company_brightloop"). OE 5 : Search emails for any inbound messages from client HR contacts about delayed payments or stipend issues, using search_emails with queries targeting Rachel Whitfield or Tessa Moreno or "delayed" or "reimbursement". OE 7 : Check BrightLoop's April relocation records in Airtable using airtable_search_records on the Relocations table with field_name "Company" and value "BrightLoop". OE 8 : Log risk engagement notes on affected CRM deals using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE". The agent should create notes on at least the Vectral and BrightLoop company records (company_ids: company_vectral and company_brightloop) documenting the stipend hold impact — which employees are affected and the risk to the client relationship.
OE 9 : Send Marcus Thorne an email asking for the client-facing timeline on the stipend holds, via send_email from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com. The email should explain which of Mina's clients are affected (Vectral Kevin Tran; BrightLoop Jordan Ekwueme), and request a timeline for when holds will be reviewed/released so Mina can set expectations with client contacts.
OE 10 : Send Tessa Moreno a check-in email via send_email from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com. OE 12 : Brief David Chen on everything via send_email from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com (or alternatively via conversations_add_message in a relevant Slack channel such as #customer-engagement, channel_id C002). outcome
 ✗✓✓✗✓✗ 50%
